value.source_basis: official Treasury closing par-yield table plus dated Reuters and CNBC market reports

text: June 23, 2026 was a normal Tuesday macro session. The verified source set shows a front-end-led Treasury rally, safe-haven dollar strength, lower crude settlements tied to reopening Strait of Hormuz flows and a U.S.-Iran sanctions waiver, and technology/semiconductor equity stress.

calculation.method: Treasury 06/23/2026 row minus 06/22/2026 row; 2s10s equals 10Y minus 2Y in basis points.

text: The official U.S. Treasury par-yield curve closed June 23, 2026 at 4.16% for 2Y, 4.50% for 10Y, and 4.94% for 30Y. Versus June 22, 2Y fell 8 bp while 10Y and 30Y each fell 1 bp, widening 2s10s from +27 bp to +34 bp.

Reuters reported that the U.S. Dollar Index rose 0.38% to 101.39 on June 23 after hitting 101.42, its highest level since May 2025, as markets repriced Fed-hike odds and a megacap stock selloff supported safe-haven dollar demand.

value.session_note: both benchmarks hit near-four-month lows during the session
drivers: investor focus on crude flows through the Strait of Hormuz
U.S. 60-day sanctions waiver after initial U.S.-Iran peace talks
Oman and Iran discussions on future Strait of Hormuz navigation administration

text: Reuters reported that Brent futures closed down 82 cents, or 1.1%, at $77.08 per barrel on June 23, while U.S. WTI futures settled 65 cents, or 0.9%, lower at $73.21 per barrel; both benchmarks hit near-four-month lows as investors watched Hormuz flows after U.S.-Iran peace-talk progress.

Reuters reported that the Nasdaq Composite was down 1.7% on June 23, wiping out around $776 billion in market value, while the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index fell 7.5%, Micron dropped 10.8%, and Nvidia declined 3.4% as technology inflows unwound amid rate concerns and AI-spending doubts.

subject.label: defensive dollar, cheaper oil, front-end rates rally

value.rates_signal: front-end-led bull steepening rather than a full long-end rally
value.fx_signal: dollar supported by Fed-hike repricing and safe-haven demand
value.oil_signal: near-four-month-lows in crude futures reduce the inflation impulse but reflect fragile conflict normalization
value.equity_signal: technology concentration and semiconductor stress carried the risk-off impulse

text: The June 23 macro regime was defensive but not a simple recession trade: oil settlements moved lower as supply-route risk eased, the dollar rose on hawkish Fed repricing and safe-haven demand, Treasury yields bull-steepened from the front end, and technology concentration stress drove the risk-off impulse.
